name: Reaves & Lee Attorneys at Law
description_short: Texas-based law firm offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing, debt negotiation, and IRS resolution with 20+ years of experience.
description_long:
Reaves & Lee, PLLC is a law firm founded on the principle of helping Texans navigate financial and personal crises. Over 20+ years, the firm has established itself as a trusted referral destination for bankruptcy and personal injury legal services across Texas. The firm explicitly markets itself as a combined practice handling both bankruptcy relief and personal injury claims, positioning this dual-focus approach as an advantage for clients facing compounded hardships.

The firm's bankruptcy services include Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing, Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing, IRS resolution, business bankruptcy, and debt negotiation. Their website emphasizes immediate creditor relief—specifically stopping wage garnishments, repossessions, and foreclosures. They offer free consultations and promise to guide clients through the filing process with clarity and personal support. The firm also maintains a secondary practice in personal injury law (car accidents, workplace injuries, slip-and-fall, wrongful death) but this profile focuses on their bankruptcy services.

Reaves & Lee distinguishes itself through several claimed strengths: dual-practice capability under one roof, promised fast action with immediate creditor relief, direct attorney access (not paralegal-only consultations), and a reputation for being attorney-referral trusted. Client testimonials emphasize feeling heard, guided through complex processes, and treated with care rather than as a case number. The firm positions itself as anti-jargon and supportive rather than transactional.

The firm's limitations are inherent to any law firm model: bankruptcy services are limited to Texas jurisdiction based on their marketing. Pricing is not disclosed on the website, requiring consultation for cost clarity. No specific information about their success rates, case timelines, or fee structures is publicly available, which means prospective clients must contact them directly for basic cost and timeline expectations.
address: 1751 River Run Suite 200, Fort Worth, TX 76107

company_info.headquarters: 1751 River Run Suite 200, Fort Worth, TX 76107

pros: Offers both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings with personalized guidance
Promises immediate action to stop wage garnishments, repossessions, and foreclosures
Provides free consultations and initial bankruptcy evaluations
Dual bankruptcy and personal injury practice allows handling of cases where financial and physical harm are linked
Explicitly positions direct attorney access (not paralegal-only service) as standard
20+ years operating history with referenced attorney referral network across Texas
Client testimonials emphasize personalized care and feeling supported through stressful process
cons: No pricing, fee structures, or payment plan information disclosed on website—cost remains unknown until consultation
No published success rates, case outcomes, or timeline expectations for bankruptcy filings
Services appear limited to Texas jurisdiction only
Combined bankruptcy and personal injury focus may dilute specialization in bankruptcy-only practices
No information about handling of complex business bankruptcies or high-asset Chapter 7 cases
best_for: Texas residents facing wage garnishment, repossession, or foreclosure who need immediate court intervention
Individuals with concurrent financial and personal injury crises seeking one-firm representation
Debtors seeking personalized guidance and attorney access rather than high-volume paralegal-driven firms
Texans with IRS tax debt or complex debt negotiation needs alongside bankruptcy filing
diagnosis: Reaves & Lee is best for Texas residents in active financial crisis (garnishment, repossession, foreclosure) who value direct attorney contact and personalized guidance. The primary caveat is that detailed pricing, fee structures, and success rate data are unavailable online—cost and timeline expectations require direct consultation before engagement.
services: Personal injury claims (car accidents, workplace injuries, wrongful death, slip-and-fall)
